content:
IVX ANDROID CRASH + CROPPED UI — FINAL ROOT-CAUSE REPAIR AND END-TO-END QA

I provided a real Android screen recording dated 2026-07-14.

Observed evidence from the recording:

1. IVX dashboard content is cropped horizontally.
2. The left side of cards, numbers and headings is outside the screen.
3. Property detail cards exceed the Android viewport width.
4. Reels opens and plays.
5. After approximately 14 seconds, IVX disappears and Android returns to the phone home screen.
6. This is a failed real-device test.
7. Do not mark Android QA, Reels QA or production certification as passed.

CURRENT CORRECT STATUS:

ANDROID REAL-DEVICE QA = FAILED
RESPONSIVE UI = FAILED
REELS SESSION = CRASHED
SCREEN RECORDING = CAPTURED
FINAL RELEASE = NOT CERTIFIED

Do not provide another narrative-only report.
Diagnose, repair, rebuild, install, test and return evidence.

==================================================
1. ATTACH THE RECORDING TO THE ACTIVE QA TASK
==================================================

Use the supplied recording as production QA evidence.

Create one failure record containing:

- test date
- device model
- Android version
- application version
- versionCode
- runtimeVersion
- GitHub SHA
- APK build ID
- crash time in recording
- active route
- active reel ID
- active property/deal ID
- screen orientation
- viewport width and height
- trace ID

Classify the failures separately:

A. Horizontal clipping / responsive layout
B. Reels or native activity crash
C. Missing real-device acceptance

Do not merge these into one generic issue.

==================================================
2. CAPTURE THE EXACT CRASH ROOT CAUSE
==================================================

Reproduce the failure using the exact final APK on a connected Android device.

Run:

adb logcat -c

Launch the IVX application, repeat the recording flow and capture:

adb logcat -v time

Filter and preserve all relevant output for:

AndroidRuntime
FATAL EXCEPTION
ReactNativeJS
ReactNative
Expo
expo-video
expo-av
ExoPlayer
MediaCodec
MediaPlayer
ActivityManager
WindowManager
OutOfMemoryError
SIGABRT
SIGSEGV
IOException
IllegalStateException
ANR
lowmemorykiller

Also collect:

adb shell dumpsys meminfo com.ivxholdings.app
adb shell dumpsys activity activities
adb shell dumpsys media.player
adb shell dumpsys package com.ivxholdings.app

Return:

- exact exception
- exact stack trace
- process exit reason
- crashing thread
- native module
- JavaScript file and line where available
- active reel ID
- media URL
- number of mounted players
- number of playing players
- memory immediately before crash
- whether Android killed the process for memory
- whether the app called exit/restart
- whether navigation incorrectly launched the home screen

Do not use “probably,” “likely” or “possible.”
Return the actual root cause proven by logs.

==================================================
3. FIX HORIZONTAL CROPPING END TO END
==================================================

Audit every screen shown in the recording:

- IVX home/dashboard
- summary metric cards
- Explore Deals
- property/deal cards
- property detail
- fractional ownership blocks
- developer information
- verification badges
- Details and Invest Now buttons
- Reels
- bottom navigation
- owner modules

Search for:

- fixed pixel widths
- width greater than screen width
- minWidth
- absolute positioning
- negative margins
- translateX
- left offsets
- horizontal scale transforms
- hard-coded desktop widths
- nested horizontal containers
- parent padding plus width: 100%
- Dimensions values cached before orientation changes
- useWindowDimensions not used
- SafeAreaInsets omitted
- unsupported landscape layout
- text with nowrap behavior
- oversized image/video aspect ratios

Required React Native rules:

- use useWindowDimensions()
- use SafeAreaView or correct safe-area insets
- card width must be availableWidth, not full width plus padding
- use flex: 1 where appropriate
- use maxWidth: "100%"
- use alignSelf: "stretch"
- avoid fixed widths for mobile cards
- use flexWrap for badges
- use numberOfLines only where intentional
- allow text to shrink/wrap safely
- no negative horizontal margins
- no horizontal ScrollView around core page content
- no overflow hidden masking real layout defects

Required width calculation:

availableWidth =
screenWidth
- safeAreaLeft
- safeAreaRight
- containerPaddingLeft
- containerPaddingRight

Every major card must fit inside availableWidth.

Test these Android widths:

- 320
- 360
- 384
- 393
- 412
- 430
- 480

Also test font scaling:

- 100%
- 115%
- 130%
- 150%

Acceptance:

- zero content clipped on left
- zero content clipped on right
- zero horizontal page scrolling
- headings remain readable
- prices and ROI remain visible
- action buttons remain fully visible
- badges wrap without overlap
- property details fit the screen
- bottom navigation remains centered
- safe areas are respected

==================================================
4. LOCK OR SUPPORT ORIENTATION CORRECTLY
==================================================

Determine whether IVX officially supports:

A. Portrait only
or
B. Portrait and landscape

If portrait only:

- configure Android and Expo orientation consistently
- prevent activity recreation loops
- prevent accidental landscape rendering
- ensure video fullscreen can rotate independently only when intended

If both orientations are supported:

- recompute dimensions after every orientation change
- rebuild card layouts
- preserve active reel
- preserve scroll position
- prevent stale width values
- prevent player recreation crashes

Return the selected policy and exact configuration files changed.

==================================================
5. REFACTOR REELS PLAYER LIFECYCLE
==================================================

Required architecture:

- exactly one actively playing video
- maximum three mounted video players
- previous, active and next only
- every other item uses a poster image
- stable keyExtractor using reel.id
- never use array index as key
- cursor pagination
- no nested vertical ScrollView
- no duplicate player instances
- pause when route loses focus
- pause when app enters background
- release players more than one item away
- clean up every listener
- cancel pending async state updates on unmount
- prevent source replacement races
- prevent multiple retry loops
- prevent repeated player creation during rapid swiping

Playback condition:

shouldPlay =
screenFocused
AND appForeground
AND activeReel
AND visibilityPercent >= 80
AND playerReady

When shouldPlay becomes false:

- pause immediately
- stop audio immediately
- do not continue playback in background

When route unmounts:

- pause
- detach listeners
- release player
- clear pending timers
- clear aborted network operations safely

==================================================
6. PROTECT THE APP FROM ONE BAD VIDEO
==================================================

One reel must never terminate the entire IVX application.

Add:

- ErrorBoundary around each reel
- ErrorBoundary around the feed route
- timeout for player initialization
- bounded retry count
- unsupported-codec handling
- expired URL refresh
- missing URL validation
- invalid duration handling
- missing poster fallback
- network-loss fallback
- corrupted-media fallback

Failed reel behavior:

- display poster
- show “Video unavailable”
- provide Retry
- allow swipe to next reel
- log exact reel ID and error
- do not crash or exit the application

==================================================
7. AUDIT MEDIA COMPATIBILITY
==================================================

For every reel currently returned by production, verify:

- media ID
- playback URL
- HTTP status
- content type
- range request support
- Content-Length
- codec
- audio codec
- dimensions
- frame rate
- duration
- file size
- orientation metadata
- signed URL expiry
- thumbnail URL
- poster URL

Use Android-compatible output such as:

- H.264 video
- AAC audio
- MP4 or supported HLS
- web-optimized metadata
- correct range requests

Do not publish unsupported or unprocessed media.

If one of the six current production videos is responsible for the crash:

- quarantine it
- preserve the database record
- mark processing status failed
- keep the remaining feed available
- reprocess it before republishing

==================================================
8. FIX MEMORY AND RESOURCE MANAGEMENT
==================================================

Measure:

- memory before opening Reels
- memory after first reel
- memory after 10 swipes
- memory after 25 swipes
- memory after 50 swipes
- memory after leaving Reels
- number of player instances at each step
- number of active audio sessions
- image cache use
- video cache use

Acceptance:

- active players = exactly 1
- mounted players <= 3
- no monotonic uncontrolled memory growth
- memory falls toward baseline after leaving Reels
- no duplicate audio
- no leaked listeners
- no leaked player objects
- no Android low-memory termination

==================================================
9. VERIFY NAVIGATION AND APP EXIT BEHAVIOR
==================================================

Audit:

- Android back handler
- Expo Router navigation
- deep links
- reel property links
- fullscreen video exit
- app state listeners
- activity recreation
- restart/reload commands
- owner reload controls
- accidental Linking.openURL calls
- accidental Intent launch
- process-exit calls
- error handler redirects
- home-screen navigation

The application must never return to the Android launcher because a reel fails.

Test:

- open Reels from home
- return to home inside IVX
- open property from reel
- return to same reel
- use Android Back
- use app bottom navigation
- background/foreground five times
- lock/unlock screen
- rotate if supported
- open and close comments
- open and close share sheet

==================================================
10. VERIFY LANDING PAGE AND NATIVE SYNC
==================================================

The same canonical reel records must drive:

- native Android Reels
- native iOS Reels
- public landing page Reels
- property detail media
- deal detail media
- Admin HQ preview

Verify:

- same reel IDs
- same property/deal relationship
- same captions
- same display order
- same published status
- same engagement counts
- no duplicate records
- no stale cached feed
- no landing-only videos
- no app-only videos unless visibility rules require it

Landing pattern:

3 property/deal cards
then 1 reel
repeat

Landing videos must:

- load poster first
- pause outside viewport
- never all autoplay
- preserve layout dimensions
- cause zero page jumping
- cause zero blank black sections

==================================================
11. BUILD A NEW ANDROID RELEASE
==================================================

After all fixes:

1. Push all approved changes to GitHub.
2. Verify local HEAD equals GitHub HEAD.
3. Run TypeScript.
4. Run lint.
5. Run unit and integration tests.
6. Run Expo Doctor.
7. Verify app configuration.
8. Increment Android versionCode.
9. Use a clean build cache.
10. Build a new release APK from the final GitHub SHA.
11. Generate SHA-256 checksum.
12. Verify embedded JavaScript bundle.
13. Verify no old fatal OTA update dependency.
14. Uninstall the previous IVX APK.
15. Install the new APK cleanly.

Return:

- repository
- branch
- final SHA
- APK build ID
- APK location/URL
- versionName
- versionCode
- runtimeVersion
- package name
- checksum
- build timestamp

Do not reuse the APK from before this crash repair.

==================================================
12. REAL ANDROID DEVICE QA
==================================================

Run on a real Android device.

Required sequence:

A. Clean installation

- uninstall old app
- install final APK
- verify package and version

B. Launch testing

- first launch in airplane mode
- first launch on Wi-Fi
- launch on cellular
- three complete cold starts
- no Expo remote-update fatal error

C. Responsive layout

- home dashboard
- property list
- deal list
- property detail
- Reels
- chat
- owner login
- Admin HQ

Confirm no horizontal clipping at any screen.

D. Reels stability

- open each available reel
- 50 consecutive swipes
- 30-minute feed session
- mute/unmute
- play/pause
- replay
- comments
- like/unlike
- share
- save
- property navigation
- background/foreground five times
- lock/unlock
- Wi-Fi to cellular transition
- temporary network loss

E. Final logs

- capture complete screen recording
- capture screenshots
- capture adb logcat
- confirm zero FATAL EXCEPTION
- confirm zero ANR
- confirm no OOM termination
- confirm application never exits to launcher

==================================================
13. FULL IVX REGRESSION
==================================================

After the Android crash fix, retest:

- member registration
- member canonical sync
- owner login
- Admin HQ
- Members
- Investors
- CRM
- Variables
- Owner AI
- chat
- properties
- deals
- Reels
- landing page
- analytics events
- push notifications
- autonomous dashboard
- dashboard exports

Verify the crash repair did not break existing functionality.

==================================================
14. AUTONOMOUS EXECUTION RULE
==================================================

Do not stop after identifying a problem.

For each failure:

1. Capture exact evidence.
2. Find the responsible code or configuration.
3. Repair it.
4. Test locally.
5. Commit.
6. Push.
7. Build.
8. Deploy.
9. Install.
10. Retest.
11. Repeat until pass.

Only request an owner action for:

- a real 401/403
- revoked or missing credential
- AWS/Apple permission that cannot be granted by the current identity
- final physical-device acceptance when no connected device is available

Do not ask for credentials by default.
Test existing secret bindings first.

==================================================
15. REQUIRED FINAL EVIDENCE
==================================================

Return one final table:

ITEM | BEFORE | ROOT CAUSE | FIX | FINAL RESULT | PASS/FAIL | EVIDENCE

Include:

1. Original recording failure
2. Crash timestamp
3. Exact stack trace
4. Process exit reason
5. Crashing module
6. Responsive width root cause
7. Files changed
8. GitHub SHA
9. Backend SHA
10. Frontend SHA
11. APK build ID
12. APK checksum
13. VersionCode
14. RuntimeVersion
15. Device model
16. Android version
17. Home layout
18. Property layout
19. Deal layout
20. Reels layout
21. Active player count
22. Mounted player count
23. Memory before test
24. Memory after 50 swipes
25. 50-swipe result
26. 30-minute result
27. Background/foreground result
28. Offline launch
29. Wi-Fi launch
30. Cellular launch
31. Registration result
32. Owner login result
33. Chat result
34. Landing sync
35. adb fatal errors
36. Screenshots
37. Screen recording
38. Remaining blocker

==================================================
FINAL VERDICT RULE
==================================================

Do not report:

COMPLETE
FIXED
VERIFIED
CERTIFIED
PRODUCTION READY
ANDROID QA PASS

until all are true:

- the cropped horizontal layout is eliminated
- IVX remains open throughout the complete session
- the app does not return to the Android launcher
- exact crash root cause is documented
- a fresh APK is installed
- one active and no more than three mounted players are proven
- 50 swipes pass
- 30-minute session passes
- no fatal adb errors
- registration works
- owner login works
- chat works
- landing/native reel IDs are synchronized
- screenshots and complete recording are attached

CURRENT FINAL STATUS:

REAL DEVICE FAILURE REPRODUCED
ANDROID QA FAILED
REELS STABILITY FAILED
RESPONSIVE LAYOUT FAILED
REPAIR AND NEW APK REQUIRED
